title: Lesson 21: Links & Multi-Page Navigation (Building Real Websites)

content.explanation:
🌐 Websites are Made of Pages

Until now, you have been building single pages.

But real websites are made of multiple pages connected together.

🔗 What are Links?

Links allow users to move from one page to another using the <a> tag.

This is how navigation between pages works in real websites like portfolios, blogs, and apps.

⚡ Real Example

  • Home → index.html
  • About → about.html
  • Contact → contact.html

Links connect everything together.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Basic Link Structure

<a href="about.html">Go to About Page</a>

📌 How it works

  • <a> = anchor tag (creates link)
  • href = destination page

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Links are the backbone of multi-page websites

👉 Without links, every page is isolated

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Personal Website

Welcome to my homepage.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Build navigation links

1. Add 3 links below the paragraph:
   - Home (index.html)
   - About (about.html)
   - Contact (contact.html)

2. Use <a> tags with href attributes

🔥 This simulates a real multi-page website
mini_challenge.hint: 👉 Example: About Each link must point to a different file

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of the <a> tag in HTML?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The <a> tag is used to create hyperlinks that connect different pages or sections of a website.

takeaways: Real websites are made of multiple connected pages
The <a> tag creates links between pages
href defines where the link goes
Without links, websites are isolated pages
Navigation is essential for user experience
